ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (Hymns) Listen to Sibelius' tune which was used as the base for our hymn. [Finlandia](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fE0RbPsC9uE)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Voices Across Time](https://voices.pitt.edu/index.htm)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Another Japanese Folk Song](https://liuchiuan.com/2014/06/25/asadoya-yunta-a-common-bond-for-okinawans-the-world-over/)
[ ] (Folk Songs) \[The appropriation of Cultures by Percival Everett]\(https://faculty.gordonstate.edu/lsanders-senu/Appropriation of Cultures by Evertt.pdf)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [An old Irish Song which might have been the inspiration for Yankee Doodle](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DGwF4Rvors0)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Another Famous Japanese folk song about a rabbit](https://www.japanese-song.com/usagi/)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [About Japanese Scales](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ny4mzbbTLAc)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [Yatsuhashi Kengyō](https://www.komuso.com/people/people.pl?person=699)
[ ] (Sol-fa: Level 1) Fun warm ups and simple songs to build: [A Cappella Lego](https://youtu.be/PDXV0Y_dVrU?si=vcbPOp4KevJa6z2B) (An Interactive Video) by Voces8.
[ ] (Composer) Go hear a symphony, concerto, or string quartet live.
[ ] (Composer) [About Figured Bass](https://youtu.be/kxqbHflQZ7g?si=x9oJ2iS_qjsF6DFV)
[ ] (Composer) [About Basso Continuo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0BLr7Yt_ehw&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=16&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB)
[ ] (Composer) [Listen to some of the first known music by Vivaldi](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=P5HHPe_W6Uo&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=5&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB0gcJCYsJAYcqIYzv)
[ ] (Composer) [Play or sing a section of Vivaldi's Gloria](https://koorpartij-oefening.nl/partituren/Partituur-Gloria-RV-589-Vivaldi.pdf)
[ ] (Composer) [Longer Article bio of Vivaldi](https://imagesofvenice.com/antonio-vivaldi-life-and-legacy/)
[ ] (Composer) [Stabat Mater, another famous early piece of Vivaldi's we didn't have time for!](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hu9pDwbryVI&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=31&t=80s&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB0gcJCYsJAYcqIYzv)
[ ] (Composer) [Notes on Stabat Mater](https://www.emmanuelmusic.org/other-notes/vivaldi-stabat-mater)
[ ] (Composer) [More about Vivaldi's Montezuma](https://web.archive.org/web/20180925180628/https://www.longbeachopera.org/archive/2009-season/motezuma)
[ ] (Composer) [Four Seasons in Pop Culture](https://www.ncco.org/news/the-four-seasons-a-pop-culture-powerhouse)
[ ] (Composer) [Four Seasons in Sand Animation](https://www.openculture.com/2015/06/vivaldis-four-seasons-brought-to-life-in-sand-animations-by-the-hungarian-artist-ferenc-cako.html)
[ ] (Composer) [Recomposed by Max Richter](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hLZ6c4ZLxUY&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=15&t=243s&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB0gcJCYsJAYcqIYzv)
[ ] (Composer) [Four Seasons of Buenos Aires](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yPFUEVqTHqo&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=16&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB)
[ ] (Composer) [Montezuma full Opera](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f1V79fR0Uuk&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=18&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB)
[ ] (Composer) [Play Concerto in A minor](https://imslp.org/wiki/Violin_Concerto_in_A_minor,_RV_356_(Vivaldi,_Antonio))
[ ] (Composer) [Read more of Vivaldi's letters!](https://anima-veneziana.narod.ru/Heller/H10_Appendix_Notes_Index_etc.pdf)
[ ] (Composer) [Vivaldi Tribute](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DIGfO2Dgc9Y&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=48&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB) (based on the Presto movement of Summer)
[ ] (Composer) [Listen to a sonata by one of Vivaldi's fellow italians who moved to China as a missionary for 36 years and wrote the first book on Western Music in Chinese.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y0ONox9Nq14)
[ ] (Composer) [Vivaldi's Women Documentary](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=efonp34pV2E)
[ ] (Composer) [The Pole who bridged east and west](https://culture.pl/en/article/wojciech-bobowski-the-pole-who-bridged-the-east-west)

[ ] (Hymns) Explore the Olney hymnbook that Cowper and Newton published! [Olney Hymns](https://books.google.com/books?id=1P5NAQAAMAAJ&pg=PR2&source=kp_read_button&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0#v=onepage&q&f=false).
[ ] (Hymns) [William Cowper's poetry](https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poets/william-cowper#tab-poems)
[ ] (Folk Songs) [More about Voyageurs](https://www.thecanadianencyclopedia.ca/en/article/voyageur)
[ ] (Sol-fa: Level 1) Fun warm ups and simple songs to build: [A Cappella Lego](https://youtu.be/PDXV0Y_dVrU?si=vcbPOp4KevJa6z2B) (An Interactive Video) by Voces8.
[ ] (Composer) Go see the Messiah (many places will perform it at Christmas and Easter). With younger kids, just the Christmas section (about an hour) may be best. But if your students are old enough, the experience of the whole piece is unmatched if you have the chance to hear it in person.
[ ] (Composer) Sing or play a line from the Messiah.
[ ] (Composer) [Another Version of the Messiah](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_l0TnItZ8JeSgnWnsbTl9xvRjVSRavsdSY)
[ ] (Composer) [Messiah Score (free)](https://www.pandolfopaolo.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/03/Handel-Messiah-English.pdf)
[ ] (Composer) [The Story of Handel's Messiah](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4YQo_pgxl8g&list=PLvg0fKfOahKR2Ev7baESVtsh0bcWwpx0v&index=22&pp=gAQBiAQB8AUB)
[ ] (Composer) [A Soulful Celebration](https://youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_lgvIiL1v2Xv1GAq0Vmq9G4uHCG352Pe30&si=avJHhyOpEWhGEmcl)
[ ] (Composer) [Documentary about Scarlatti](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g2zrOksZRko)
[ ] (Composer) [Secrets of Andalusia](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=OLAK5uy_l_SfG2DkxJiAhWfa0LOHfA-obUHkjePRA)
[ ] (Composer) [Learn more about the differences between the Clavichord, Harpsichord, and Fortepiano, and Modern Piano.](https://www.openculture.com/2019/02/how-the-clavichord-harpsichord-became-the-modern-piano.html)
[ ] (Composer) [Amy Beach dedicated this piece to her cat, Hamlet, saying ](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fZcam3H4jfE)"the composer is indebted to 'Hamlet', a large black Angora who had been placed on the keyboard with the hope that he might emulate Scarlatti's cat and improvise a fugue theme."
[ ] (Composer) [List of works by Scarlatti (with free scores)](https://imslp.org/wiki/List_of_works_by_Domenico_Scarlatti)
[ ] (Composer) [Enterprising Women Podcast](https://salonera.org/podcast/enterprising-women/) (talks about some composers influenced by Scarlatti)

resources.title: 150 American Folk Songs to Sing, Read, and Play
resources.author: Peter Erdei (editor), Katalin Komolos (composer)
resources.rationale: Good collection of American folk songs for younger children. Includes some games and sol-fa notes.

resources.title: Before Music: Where Instruments Come From
resources.author: Annette Bay Pimentel (author), Madison Safer (illustrator)

resources.title: Child's Own Book of Great Musician Series
resources.author: Thomas Tapper

resources.title: Child's Own Book of Great Musicians: Handel, Haydn, and Liszt
resources.author: Thomas Tapper and RaeAnna Goss

resources.title: Handel, Who Knew What He Liked
resources.author: M. T. Anderson (author), Kevin Hawkes (illustrator)

resources.title: I, Vivaldi
resources.author: Janice Shefelman (author), Tom Shefelman (illustrator)
resources.rationale: A picture book inspired by the life of Antonio Vivaldi.

resources.title: Mama Lisa's World
resources.author: Mama Lisa
resources.rationale: Free site of folk songs from around the world. Often includes recordings, sheet music, and lyrics (Note: There will be discrepancies among the versions).

resources.title: More Stories of Composers for Young Musicians
resources.author: Catherine Wolff

resources.title: Musical Instruments of the World: An Illustrated Encyclopedia with more than 4000 original drawings
resources.author: The Diagram Group
resources.rationale: Great for book of centuries and history charts.

resources.title: The Complete Book of Great Musicians: Handel
resources.author: Percy A. Scholes

resources.title: The Complete Book of the Great Musicians
resources.author: Percy A. Scholes
resources.rationale: Older book in friendly narrative style about many great composers throughout history. Selections used in lessons.
